observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to compute and provide for depreciation on the Public Infrastructure assets to present faithfully the balances of the said accounts as of September 30, 2023. Thereafter, submit a copy of the lapsing schedule to the Office of the Audit Team for reference.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant will look for the guidelines on the depreciation of Public Infrastructure and will compute and provide for depreciation on the Public Infrastructure assets followed by DPWH but it has separate guidelines, still waiting for the circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor; a. Explore strategies in coordination with the HRMO to fill the open plantilla items at the Accounting Office ensuring to hire eligible personnel to augment the Accounting Office workforce; b. Direct the Accountant to ensure that personnel in the Accounting Office particularly those involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts to turnover the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor; explore strategies in coordination with the HRMO to fill the open plantilla items at the Accounting Office ensuring to hire eligible personnel to augment the Accounting Office workforce; Municipal Accountant will ensure that personnel in the Accounting Office particularly those involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts to turnover with the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.reason: Lack qualified personnel; cannot comply with the ten (10) days reglamentary period due to lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management that the BAC to prepare PMR in the form prescribed by the GPPB and submit to the HOPE for approval and submission to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: BAC will prepare PMR and submit to the HOPE for approval and submission to the GPPB

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Compute and provide for depreciation on the PPEs to present faithfully the balances of the said accounts; and
Present the related accumulated depreciation for each PPE, instead of consolidating amounts computed in one account.
Provide and submit a copy of the lapsing schedule to the Office of the Audit Team for reference.

We recommended and the management agreed to facilitate the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE by performing the following:
Identify other departments within the LGU that may have available staff with relevant skills (e.g., record-keeping, accounting, or logistics) who can be temporarily reassigned to assist and focus with the inventory process.
Collaborate with other government agencies that can provide training on inventory management, especially on the full implementation of the 'One-time Cleansing of PPE Accounts,' as outlined in COA Circular 2020-006, so that the LGU may be fully aware of the primary guidelines of the said circular.
Create  a new set of Inventory Committee, with members temporarily relieved of their regular duties until the completion of the physical inventory within a clear and agreed-upon timeline.
Ensure that the newly reconstituted Inventory Committee prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking process.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Ensure that PPAs funded from the 20% EDF are implemented within the timeframe specified in the AIP, with concerted efforts to fast-track unimplemented or ongoing PPAs, and set clear timelines for all projects to track progress effectively, while conducting regular progress reviews and monitoring to identify issues early and prevent delays in future implementations;
Prioritize hiring additional staff or engaging temporary personnel to address the manpower shortage in the Engineering Office, ensuring the engineering team is better equipped to efficiently handle the increasing number of projects; and
Provide quarterly reporting updates on ongoing projects and the utilization of the Development Fund as certified by the Municipal Engineering Office to enhance transparency, hold stakeholders accountable, and ensure funds are used properly while addressing delays promptly.

We recommended and the management through the Municipal Treasurer agreed to strengthen the control measures such as immediately depositing collections to the authorized bank to prevent accumulation of the undeposited collections in compliance with Section 62 (C) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended and the management agreed to ensure that the LSB streamline the reporting and monitoring process of the SEF Budget with the LSB furnishing a copy of the quarterly and annual SEF utilization to the Sanggunian Bayan, DepEd DO and the DBM Regional Office.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure:
Through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) prepare an updated LDRRMFIP to incorporate all the previous years’ unutilized PPAs and be made available for implementation.
That the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the adjusting journal entries to revert DRRMF aged five years and above to the General Fund.
Monitor the DRRMF in the STF by timely preparing the utilization report for use of the MDRRMO and the MDRRM Council in planning the disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LGU’s Inventory Team fasttrack and complete the PPE inventory count to verify the existence of the LGU’s properties and submit the required report to the Audit Team. Moreover, the inventory report should be reconciled with accounting and property records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated.
observations.managementAction: GSO/Inventory Team will fast track and complete the PPE inventory count to verify the existence of the LGU’s properties and submit the required report to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Avoid the practice of arbitrarily selecting the laborers and workers in a payroll system; and for future projects will comply with the BAC guidelines Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 18-2006.
observations.managementAction: Management will avoid the practice of arbitrarily selecting the laborers and workers in a payroll system; and for future projects will comply with the BAC guidelines Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 18-2006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to: a. Exert further effort in the processing of the Certificate of Title of the untitled parcels of land as required by PD 1445; b. Gradually allocate funds needed to process the titling; and c. Direct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate and supervise the proceedings of land titling and to cause the immediate transfer of the parcels of land to the Municipality. d. Ensure that all future land acquisitions, whether through purchase or donation are titled in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert further effort in the processing of the Certificate of Title of the untitled parcels of land as required by PD 1445; three(3) parcels of loand are already covered with TCTs. Gradually allocate funds needed to process the titling; Municipal Assessor to facilitate and supervise the proceedings of land titling and to cause the immediate transfer of the parcels of land to the Municipality. Ensure that all future land acquisitions, whether through purchase or donation are titled in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Take into consideration the imposition of liquidated damages for those projects that meets the conditions as specified in Item 9.1, Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 (Updated as of 15 April 2023); and
observations.managementAction: Management will take into consideration the imposition of liquidated damages for those projects that meets the conditions as specified in Item 9.1, Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 (Updated as of 15 April 2023).

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Cease and discontinue the practice of consuming fuel for government motor transportation without first undergoing the process of government procurement and disbursement; b. Consider the conduct of EPA for fuel and gasoline so that the operations of the LGU are not hampered due to inadequate supplies at the beginning of the year; and,
observations.managementAction: Management will cease and discontinue the practice of consuming fuel for government motor transportation without first undergoing the process of government procurement and disbursement; and consider the conduct of EPA for fuel and gasoline so that the operations of the LGU are not hampered due to inadequate supplies at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct and ensure that the GAD Focal Person prepare and establish the GAD budget by attribution, integrating the GAD perspective in major programs as a means of gradually increasing the allocation on GAD Budget and the gender responsiveness of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Management prefers the allocation of 5% GAD rather than attribution to ensure we comply the 5% GAD requirement

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor convey the issue of the General Revision of Real Property Assessments and Classification to the Provincial level, convincing and enforcing the latter to adhere the Section 219 of RA 7160 to generate additional revenue from the increased collection of real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: Assessor convey the issue of the General Revision of Real Property Assessments and Classification to the Provincial level, convincing and enforcing the latter to adhere the Section 219 of RA 7160 to generate additional revenue from the increased collection of real property taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to exerteffortinretrievingtheallegedtransmittal letters or Liquidation Reports duly stamped “received” by the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU) and furnish a copy to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accounting Office retrieve the alleged transmittal letters or Liquidation Reports duly stamped “received” by the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU) and furnish a copy to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in establishing the Municipal Reading Center.
observations.managementAction: Concerned Officials shall establish a Municipal Reading Center.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that; a. The Accounting Office apply the criteria on PPE costing P50,000.00 and below and make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts and ensure proper classification of assets in the books; b. GSO Designate update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of the Circular; c. Moreover, use the Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for issuance of semi-expandable items to establish monitoring, control, and accountability over them.
observations.managementAction: Accounting office to sort the items classified as tangible items costing below the capitalization threshold of P50,000.00 and below and make the necessary adjustments in the books. GSO is still in the process required to submit the list of PPE including its accumulated depreciation.
observations.reason: GSO is still in the process required to submit the list of PPE including its accumulated depreciation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor convey the issue of the General Revision of Real Property Assessments and Classification to the Provincial level, convincing and enforcing the latter to adhere to Section 219 of RA 7160 to generate additional revenue from the increased collection of real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Province of Zamboanga Sibugay is still in the process of conveying and enforcing the latter to adhere to Section 219 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: The Province of Zamboanga Sibugay is still in the process of conveyance and enforcement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU’s Inventory Team fast track and complete the PPE inventory count to verify the existence of the LGU’s properties and submit the required report to the Audit Team. Moreover, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to compute and provide for depreciation on the Public Infrastructure assets to present faithfully the balances of the said accounts as of September 30, 2023. Thereafter, submit a copy of the lapsing schedule to the Office of the Audit Team for reference.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze, review, reconcile and monitor all payables and adjust the books to ensure that only valid payable were recorded in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Properly disclose the implementation of the above-mentioned resolution along with EO 138, their details, financial, and non-financial effects to the LGU;
Revise the account name of the account “Share from Internal Revenue Allotment” to “Share from National Tax Allotment” in compliance with the above-mentioned resolution and EO; and
Moving forward, to study constantly the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC) publications, COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Avoid the practice of arbitrarily selecting the laborers and workers in a payroll system; and
For future projects to be undertaken by administration, comply with the guidelines Annex “A” of GPPB Resolution N0. 18-2006 notably on the submission of the following documents, to wit:
Name of Project Facilitators per project and related documents that would prove his/her appointment as such duly approved by the Municipal Mayor.
Certification or other related document that a formation or creation of Pakyaw Group was conducted.
Letter of undertaking or application of Pakyaw Group to enter into Pakyaw Contract with the Municipality.
Detailed information of each individual/workers members of each Pakyaw Group for each Pakyaw Contract.

We recommended that Management to:
Exert further effort in the processing of the Certificate of Title of the untitled parcels of land as required by PD 1445;
Gradually allocate funds needed to process the titling; and
Direct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate and supervise the proceedings of land titling and to cause the immediate transfer of the parcels of land to the Municipality.
Ensure that all future land acquisitions, whether through purchase or donation are titled in the name of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Take into consideration the imposition of liquidated damages for those projects that meets the conditions as specified in Item 9.1, Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 (Updated as of 15 April 2023); and
Strictly follow and monitor the project timeliness and ensure prompt implementation of the projects. Perform an inspection to ascertain the current status of the project, assess and identify the magnitude of any damage or loss, take necessary measures to ensure the prompt completion of the project, in order for the intended recipients to promptly benefit from the projects.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office appropriately monitor and limit the payroll deduction to the order of preference provided in the GAA to ensure that net take home pay shall not be less than P5,000.00.
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the maximum utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of all planned GAD-related programs and projects during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct and ensure that the GAD Focal Person prepare and establish the GAD budget by attribution, integrating the GAD perspective in major programs as a means of gradually increasing the allocation on GAD Budget and the gender responsiveness of the agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the HRMO to explore additional methods of informing the public about the vacant positions in the IAS office, such as publishing in a widely circulated newspaper or making an announcement through a radio station.
Designate personnel/s to serve as Internal Audit Service/Unit and direct the IAS Officer to align its activities with the provisions of PGIAM while carrying out its mandated functions.

We recommended that Management to exercise due prudence in the preparation of the detailed estimates for the Program of Works and Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) for the upcoming projects to in line with the COA cost estimates.

We recommended the BAC/BAC Secretariat prepare PMR in the form prescribed by the GPPB and submit to the HOPE for approval and submit it to GPPB as prescribed. The BAC should likewise post the PMR in accordance with E.O. 662, s. 2007, as amended.
observations.managementAction: The PMR has been prepared by the BAC/BAC Secretariat. However, evidence to show that the same was submitted to the GPPB is the prescribed.
observations.reason: However, evidence to show that the same was submitted to the GPPB is the prescribed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management thru the LDDRMO will formulate its PSCP to ensure that the agency’s operations can continue and the potential adverse effects arising from disruptive events be mitigated.
observations.managementAction: LDDRMO to formulate its PSCP to ensure that the agency’s operations can continue and the potential adverse effects arising from disruptive events be mitigated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card (Appendix 57 of GAM for LGUs) and Local Road Network Ledger Card (Appendix 63 of GAM for LGUs) and Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (Appendix 63 of GAM for LGUs) in coordination with the Office MPDC.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card (Appendix 57 of GAM for LGUs) and Local Road Network Ledger Card and Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (Appendix 63 of GAM for LGUs) in coordination with the Office MPDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to exert effort in retrieving the alleged unliquidated funds by communicating with ICFAU and follow-up the transmittal letters or status of their liquidations submitted and furnish a copy to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Management will communicate with ICFAU and follow-up the status of their liquidations submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to institutionalize GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation, that could either be manually operated.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Person and Budget office to institutionalize GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU will establish concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in small portion in establishing the Municipal Reading Center.
observations.managementAction: There is no municipal reading center yet. However, a portion in the municipality was allocated for the purpose.
observations.reason: There is no municipal reading center yet. However, a portion in the municipality was allocated for the purpose.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the GSO to complete the PPE inventory count to prove the existence of the LGU’s properties and to submit the required report to the Auditor. Also, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustments should be made where the error was committed; and
Effect the necessary reclassification of the unserviceable to its appropriate asset account.

Recommendations
We recommended that:
GSO prepares the Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment
Engineering office conduct inspection and certify that the above-mentioned structures are indeed non-existing to date.
Sangguniang Bayan adopt a resolution to drop the above properties in the books of the LGU.
Municipal accountant prepares a Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize/drop the asset from the books of account and recognize loss based on their total book value.

Recommendations
We recommended that;
The Accounting Office apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE and make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts and ensure proper classification of assets in the books; and
GSO Designate update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of the Circular. Moreover, use the Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for issuance of semi-expandable items to establish monitoring, control, and accountability over them.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that the concerned officials look into the process and establish proper control and monitoring mechanism regarding the cost of repairs paid the tenants in order to improve the LGU’s collection system.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that reconciliation and verification of the affected accounts should be made and prepare appropriate adjusting entries.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to uphold the policy on the acquisition of Motor Vehicles enunciated in Section 9.1 of Budget Circular No. 2022-01 dated February 11, 2022.

Recommendation
We recommended that management prepare and submit the MRL either upon the submission of annual financial statements or prior to our auditor's report.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to use the certification of expenses not requiring receipts for expenses P300.00 and below and RER for expenses exceeding P300.00.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant come up with an agreed time frame in undertaking their respective activities and responsibilities as mentioned in Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382, to avoid delays in the remittance of RPT Collections.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to uphold the policy on the utilization of POP set forth under COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015, particularly on the identification of programs/projects chargeable to the Peace and Order Program.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs, projects, and activities to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer or the person in charge of the heavy equipment to prepare the ERRO which will be presented to the Treasurer’s office before payment. Moreover, the municipal engineer’s higher estimated rental rate may be considered in their succeeding Revised Revenue Code.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC to prepare PMR in the form prescribed by the GPPB and submit to the HOPE for approval and submission to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 days after the end of each semester. The BAC should likewise post the PMR in accordance with E.O. 662, s. 2007, as amended.

We recommended that the municipal
accountant exert extra effort to locate the
documents supporting the reconciling item. Be
it noted that the longer the reconciling items
remained unaccounted, the lesser its
chances to be corrected for reasons
previously cited.

We recommended that management return the
unexpended balance of P220,000.00 by
coordinating with source agency or
Department of Budget and Management (DBM)
and BTr.

We recommended that concerned municipal
officials to comply with the rules and
regulations on the submission of the
disbursement vouchers together with the
supporting documents, official receipts,
monthly trial balances and bank
reconciliation statements.

We recommended that the concerned
municipal officials to comply with
Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No.
2017-95 dated August 9, 2017, by
appropriating the necessary funds in
establishing the Municipal Reading
Center.

We recommended that management:
Create an Inventory committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory count. The inventory committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Treasury Office. They are required to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned; and
Check the condition of the property. Any damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Report and immediately request for disposal. Lastly, the inventory reports should be reconciled with the accounting records to determine the correctness of the property records.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance in the financial statements as of any given period of time. Likewise, instruct the accountant to use the Municipal Treasures Office certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due for the year in setting up the real Property Tax/Special Education Tax receivable and deferred income at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer timely submit the list of delinquent Business Taxpayers to the Municipal accountant to be used in the computation and recording of the business taxes. The Municipal Accountant record the business taxes earned during the period by drawing the following entries:
Debit Other Receivables 			P93,589.43
Credit	Business Tax 				P 86,631.36
Prior Year Adjustment			       6,958.07
The Municipal Accountant record the business taxes earned for the next period using the following entries:
Debit Other Receivables 			  XXX
Credit	Business Tax 				  XXX

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor convey the issue of the  General Revision of Real Property Assessments and Classification to the Provincial level, convincing and enforcing the latter to adhere the Section 219 of RA 7160 to generate additional revenue from the increased collection of real property taxes.

We recommended that all programs and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specifics as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken. Henceforth, there should be no lump sum appropriation order to promote the policy of transparency in the budget.

We recommended that Management strictly follow and monitor the project timeliness and ensure prompt implementation of the projects. Moreover, conduct inspection to determine the status of the project, evaluate and determine the extent of damage or loss, take appropriate action to cause the immediate completion of the project, and enforce legal action against the contractor for the damages to recover the amount of losses, if any.

We recommended that concerned LGU officials and employees headed by the LCE himself fully implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013 - 004 dated January 30, 2013, on all its on-going and/or about to start Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) to inform and notify the public of the essential features/significance/elements of the LGU’s PPAs as required per regulation for purposes of transparency and good governance.

We recommended that Management:
Submit a written explanation and detailed justification on the cost discrepancy and deficiencies noted in the Technical Evaluation Report; and
Exercise due prudence in the preparation of the detailed estimates for the Program of Works and Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) of all projects.

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the LCPC funds by appropriate planning and assisting in the office of the MSWDO in terms of the implementation by providing training and learning activities to its staff to expedite the implementation of LCPC’s PPAs.

We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.
We recommended that Management submit copies of the perfected PO’s and Notices of Deliveries as prescribed in the COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.
We recommended that Management thru the LDDRMO formulate its PSCP to ensure that the agency’s operations can continue and the potential adverse effects arising from disruptive events be mitigated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended that Management expedite the preparation of necessary documents relative to the procurement of Food Supplies and the procurement of the needed disaster and rescue equipment in order to improve the capacity of the LGU to respond to emergencies and disasters.
We recommended that the BAC Chairman directs the BAC Secretariat to send written notice to losing bidders notifying the bidding results and the recommendation of award of the contract.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman directs the BAC Secretariat to comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement. Notices of Award and Proceed, and the approved contract be posted in the PhilGEPS website and website of the municipality, if any, within the required period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to have a legal expert to study the legality of Sanguniang Panlalawigan  and Sanguniang Bayan Ordinances issued by their respective Sanguniang Members  before implementing it in the LGU in order to prevent future misinterpretations.

Municipal Treasurer  and the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate each other
for the strict compliance of the
above cited regulation for reliable and accurate
account balance in the financial
statements as of any given period of time.
Likewise, instruct the accountant to use
the Municipal Treasures Office
certified list of taxpayers showing the
amount due for the year in setting up
the real Property Tax/Special Education
Tax receivable and deferred income at
the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal
Treasurer review and verify the Report of
Collections and Deposits and ensure
that these are properly filled-up
before submitting the same to the Audit
Team to properly serve its purpose by
providing the needed accounting and
financial information to prospective users
at any given point in time. Moreover,
observe the proper frequency of
deposit/remittance in conformity of DBM-DOF
Joint Cir. 1-81, dated January 1, 1998.

We recommended that management require
the Municipal Accountant and/or HRM
Section to submit to the Audit Team the
lacking documents in order to facilitate
proper review of TLB payments; and
henceforth, ensure that claim for TLB is
duly supported with the necessary
documents required under the said COA
Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to submit to the COA
the mentioned 25 cancelled checks for
validation and henceforth, ensure that all
cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and
reported chronologically with the other checks
issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly
indicated therein.

We recommended that the LCPC focal person
religiously provide the complete
documentation in supporting the LCPC’s
disbursements and the Municipal Accountant
refrain from processing claims without
complete and correct documentation.

We recommended that management have
adequate planning of the work activities
in order to avoid unnecessary
expenditures; and to instruct the
Accounting Unit to strictly observe item
5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the LSB Chairman stop
the practice of paying from the SEF
expenses which are not allowable and
include allocations from their SEF for
the ECCD PPAs.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards
Committee carefully and judiciously review
and evaluate all documents relative to the
procurement of goods before any award of
contract be made in order to ensure that
the amount paid commensurate with the
value of the materials/equipment actually
delivered and accepted.

We recommended that management adhere to
the provisions of Section 119 of P.D.
1445 and Section 322 of R.A. 7160 in the
use of appropriations to prevent the
incurrence of irregular expenditures and to
avoid disallowance in audit of the
subsequent transactions.

We recommended that the management
instruct their canvassers to require the
prospective suppliers to indicate the
brand names and other detailed item
specifications. Moreover, Direct the
General Services Unit to prepare all the
purchase orders entered into by the LGU and
accomplishes properly the P.O.s including
the brand names and other detailed
specifications of goods to be procured

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person institutionalize GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation, that could either be manually operated or developed through software.

We recommended that management thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office include in theLDRRM Investment Plan, the establishment of toilets as well asan appropriate storage area in their designated evacuation center, and establish alternative sources of water supply for the evacuation center in compliance with the minimum standards and to further improve the LGUs capabilities to be disaster resilient.

We recommended that the municipal accountant exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the reconciling item. Be it noted that the longer the reconciling items remained unaccounted, the lesser its chances to be corrected for reasons previously cited.
We recommended that management return the unexpended balance of P220,000.00 by coordinating with source agency or Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and BTr.

We recommended that concerned municipal officials to comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of the disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents, official receipts, monthly trial balances and bank reconciliation statements.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in establishing the Municipal Reading Center.

We recommended that management:
Create an Inventory committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory count. The inventory committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Treasury Office. They are required to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned; and
Check the condition of the property. Any damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Report and immediately request for disposal. Lastly, the inventory reports should be reconciled with the accounting records to determine the correctness of the property records.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance in the financial statements as of any given period of time. Likewise, instruct the accountant to use the Municipal Treasures Office certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due for the year in setting up the real Property Tax/Special Education Tax receivable and deferred income at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately the Cash in Vault balance of P2,338,974.56; and thereafter, deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank as often as necessary.

We recommended that management direct:
The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the inventories and to prepare Report of Physical Count of Inventories; and the Property/Supply Officer to maintain stock cards to record purchases and issuance of Inventories and the preparation and submission of RSMI to the accounting Office to support the dropping from the books of all issued/consumed supplies and materials. The RSMI should be regularly prepared on a monthly basis to cover issuance of inventory items for the month to avoid accumulation of unrecorded issuance.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card (Appendix 57 of GAM for LGUs) and Local Road Network Ledger Card and Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (Appendix 63 of GAM for LGUs). The Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer review and verify the Report of Collections and Deposits and ensure that these are properly filled-up before submitting the same to the Audit Team to properly serve its purpose by providing the needed accounting and financial information to prospective users at any given point in time. Moreover, observe the proper frequency of deposit/remittance in conformity of DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81, dated January 1, 1998.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and/or HRM Section to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents in order to facilitate proper review of TLB payments; and henceforth, ensure that claim for TLB is duly supported with the necessary documents required under the said COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit to the COA the mentioned 25 cancelled checks for validation and henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein.

We recommended that the LCPC focal person religiously provide the complete documentation in supporting the LCPC’s disbursements and the Municipal Accountant refrain from processing claims without complete and correct documentation.

We recommended that management have adequate planning of the work activities in order to avoid unnecessary expenditures; and to instruct the Accounting Unit to strictly observe item 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that the LSB Chairman stop the practice of paying from the SEF expenses which are not allowable and include allocations from their SEF for the ECCD PPAs.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee carefully and judiciously review and evaluate all documents relative to the procurement of goods before any award of contract be made in order to ensure that the amount paid commensurate with the value of the materials/equipment actually delivered and accepted.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of Section 119 of P.D. 1445 and Section 322 of R.A. 7160 in the use of appropriations to prevent the incurrence of irregular expenditures and to avoid disallowance in audit of the subsequent transactions.

We recommended that the management instruct their canvassers to require the prospective suppliers to indicate the brand names and other detailed item specifications. Moreover, Direct the General Services Unit to prepare all the purchase orders entered into by the LGU and accomplishes properly the P.O.s including the brand names and other detailed specifications of goods to be procured in order to serve the purpose for which the purchase order was prescribed.

We recommended management to exert effort in retrieving the alleged transmittal letters or Liquidation Reports duly stamped “received” by the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU) and furnish a copy to the audit team. Likewise, communicate with ICFAU and follow-up the status of their liquidations submitted.

We recommended that the municipal mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance in the financial statements as of any given time. Instruct the accountant to use the Municipal Treasurer’s Office certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due for the year in setting up the real Property Tax/Special Education Tax receivable and at the beginning of the year as required under Section 20, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Personnel and all concerned Punong Barangay (PB) to establish immediately an active VAW Help Desk in their respective barangays managed by a person designated by PB and staffed with competent personnel, situated within the premises of the Barangay.
We recommended that management follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-02.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to effect without delay the transfer of the much needed funds to the 14 barangays concerned relative to their 50% share of community tax or cedula and 25% share on RPT collections as mandated.

We recommended that management not to authorize deductions from the salaries of the   employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 5,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as stated in Section 52 of RA 11260.

We recommended the Management to process the titling of the land to prove claims of ownership on the real properties and to safeguard government interest. Allocate funds for the titling of the municipal lots.

We recommended Management to:
Create a team to conduct an immediate physical count of all its property, plant and equipment. This committee may be composed of the heads or representatives from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Engineer, and a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor. They are required to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 490 of GAAM, Volume I;
Further, the committee should be required to check the condition of the property. Any damage, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Report and immediately request for disposal. Lastly, the inventory reports should be reconciled with the accounting records to determine the correctness of the property records; and
Require each Department Head to conduct every year a physical inventory count of all properties under their office and to submit inventory report to the inventory committee for the random validation of the existence of the property.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the following suggested measures:
Deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank as often as necessary; and
Stop immediately the practice of holding large amount of undeposited collections depriving the municipality and its constituent’s immediate delivery of basic services.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance in the financial statements as of any given time. Instruct the accountant to use the Municipal Treasurer’s Office certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due for the year in setting up the real Property Tax/Special Education Tax receivable and at the beginning of the year as required under Section 20, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person institutionalize GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation, that could either be manually operated or developed through software.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Personnel and all concerned Punong Barangay (PB) to establish immediately an active VAW Help Desk in their respective barangays managed by a person designated by PB and staffed with competent personnel, situated within the premises of the Barangay.
We recommended that management follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-02.

We recommended that management thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office include in theLDRRM Investment Plan, the establishment of toilets as well asan appropriate storage area in their designated evacuation center, and establish alternative sources of water supply for the evacuation center in compliance with the minimum standards and to further improve the LGUs capabilities to be disaster resilient.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to effect without delay the transfer of the much needed funds to the 14 barangays concerned relative to their 50% share of community tax or cedula and 25% share on RPT collections as mandated.

We recommended that management not to authorize deductions from the salaries of the   employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 5,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as stated in Section 52 of RA 11260.

We recommended the Management to create a team to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE. This committee may be composed of the heads or representatives from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Engineer, and a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor. They are required to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance as reflected in the books of the local government unit as of any given period of time.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended the municipal accountant to exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the reconciling item. Be it noted that the longer the reconciling items remained unaccounted, the lesser its chances to be corrected for reasons previously cited.
Management Comment
The municipal accountant promised to send a letter to the depository bank requesting for information/documents on the said reconciling items.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
While we appreciate management’s promise to send a letter to the depository bank requesting copies of documents on the reconciling items, we want to emphasize the fact that any action that should be done on the matter should be made out of urgency considering that the reconciling items had been existing for more than 20 years already where the needed documents may no longer be available.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct periodic review to ensure that the Accounts Payable account consists only of valid and legitimate claims against the Municipal Government; and
Revert to the  General Fund the accounts payable which have been outstanding for two years or more against which no actual claim, has been filed or which are not covered by perfected contract on records;
Management Comment
Management promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation of the affected accounts and draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the appropriate adjusting entries.
Management Comment
The Municipal Accountant promised to correct the error immediately.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan for the creation and installation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS) directly under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to comply with the requirement of AO Nos. 278 and 70 and ensure the quality of performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations.
Management Comment
The municipal mayor expressed his desire to create the IAS, but unfortunately there are no qualified personnel or applicants for the position.
Auditor’s rejoinder
Temporarily designate personnel that would perform the function of IAS.

Recommendation
We recommended that management to follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-02.
Management Comment
The LDRRM Officer promised to prioritize items to be funded out of the LDRRMF in the ensuing years.

Recommendation
We recommended that management to closely monitor the fund utilization and anticipate possible issues for the early introduction of remedial interventions to address unwarranted delays in the implementation of the program.
Management Comment
The management has not yet commented this observation.

Recommendation
We recommended that management return the unexpended balance of P220,000.00 by coordinating with source agency or Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and BTr.
Management Comment
Management promised to issue a check and immediately deposit it to the BTr’s account.

Recommendation
We recommended the Management to process the titling of the land to prove claims of ownership on the real properties and to safeguard government interest. Allocate funds for the titling of the municipal lots.
Management Comment
Management promised to initiate the titling of Lands in the ensuing years.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the use of the Revised Chart of Accounts. Further, to verify the status and reconcile the balances with the GSIS and BIR to effect the necessary adjustments of the accounts. Furthermore, we recommend the write-off of these dormant accounts after thorough verification.

We recommend that concerned municipal officials to comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of the disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents, official receipts, monthly trial balances and bank reconciliation statements.
observations.managementAction: Some reports were submitted on time

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-02.

We recommend that concerned LGU officials and employees should always be aware and committed to implement all applicable laws, rules and regulations in the granting as well as the utilization of cash advances to avoid possible disciplinary actions or sanctions in the future for non-compliance thereof. Issue a demand letter relative to the past due accounts to remind the accountable persons of their duties to liquidate their advances. We also require the submission of copies of the transmittal letters duly received by COA Central Office on the liquidation of the intelligence fund.
observations.managementAction: Some have already liquidated. The remaining balance were more on the cash advances for confidential fund

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LCPC as provided in DILG Circular 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and OIC- SWMFP to involve closely the barangay officials in solid wastes management and activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees for the strict enforcement and continual implementation of the existing environmental laws and consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 13 barangays which have remained non-compliant in establishing MRFs. Moreover, continuously intensify information campaign and educate the constituents on the necessity of the establishment of MRF in every barangay.

We recommend that management not to authorize deductions from the salaries of the employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P4,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as shown above.
We recommend that management fast-track the implementation of the projects by closely monitoring the timely implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Out of the 12 projects, only 3 projects remained unimplemented as of March 31, 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommend that concerned management officials to religiously implement the above cited regulations on the conduct of periodic physical inventory of the LGU’s PPE as well as to appropriately reconcile the related account balances recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the use of the Revised Chart of Accounts. Further, to verify the status and reconcile the balances with the GSIS and BIR to effect the necessary adjustments of the accounts. Furthermore, we recommend the write-off of these dormant accounts after thorough verification.

We recommend the concerned municipal officials to comply with COA Circular No. 2017-019 dated October 19, 2017, by immediately withdrawing the deposits made to PNB and depositing the same with the above listed authorized depository banks.

We recommend that concerned municipal officials to comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of the disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents, official receipts, monthly trial balances and bank reconciliation statements.

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials to comply with Republic Act No. 7743 and DILG MC No. 2017-95, by appropriating the necessary funds in establishing the Municipal Reading Center. The municipal mayor shall also supervise the Punong Barangay’s compliance in establishing the Barangay Reading Center.
We recommend that management to follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-02.

We recommend that concerned LGU officials and employees should always be aware and committed to implement all applicable laws, rules and regulations in the granting as well as the utilization of cash advances to avoid possible disciplinary actions or sanctions in the future for non-compliance thereof. Issue a demand letter relative to the past due accounts to remind the accountable persons of their duties to liquidate their advances. We also require the submission of copies of the transmittal letters duly received by COA Central Office on the liquidation of the intelligence fund.
We recommend that management to follow the guidelines in the utilization of the LCPC as provided in DILG Circular 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and OIC- SWMFP to involve closely the barangay officials in solid wastes management and activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees for the strict enforcement and continual implementation of the existing environmental laws and consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 13 barangays which have remained non-compliant in establishing MRFs. Moreover, continuously intensify information campaign and educate the constituents on the necessity of the establishment of MRF in every barangay.

We recommend that management not to authorize deductions from the salaries of the   employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P 4,000.00. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as shown above.
We recommend that management fast-track the implementation of the projects by closely monitoring the timely implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other for the strict compliance of the above cited regulation for reliable and accurate account balance as reflected in the books of the local government unit as of any given period of time.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of the reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of their balances.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to timely submit the above cited RCIs, RODs, ROCs and the related supporting documents for all funds to the Municipal Accountant for the timely recording in the books of account and timely preparation by the latter of the financial statements, bank reconciliation statements and other related periodic reports for submission to the Auditor and other government regulatory bodies.

We recommend that concerned LGU officials and employees headed by the LCE himself to fully implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013 - 004 dated January 30, 2013 on all its on-going and/or about to start Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) to inform and notify the public of the essential features/significance/elements of the LGU’s PPAs as required per regulation for purposes of transparency and good governance.  Furthermore, hold or suspend any payment related to all contractors who failed to comply with this regulation to make them aware of their responsibilities after entering into contract with the government in undertaking and implementing government projects.
We recommend that the property officer to renew all Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment in accordance with the instructions under the COA Handbook on Property and Supply Management System.

We recommend that concerned management officials and employees to implement strictly the above cited regulation to avoid possible suspensions in audit or facing the consequences for its non-compliance.

We recommend that the Disbursing Officer to strictly comply with all applicable laws, rules and regulations in the liquidation of his cash advances to avoid suspensions in audit and the possibility of facing problem in the future where some claimants may deny that they were already paid of their claims.

We recommend that the concerned LGU officials and employees to strictly comply to the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-382  dated July 3, 1992 to avoid problems in the future due to possible loss, misappropriation and misuse of government funds and resources.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to fully implement strictly all applicable laws, rules and regulations in the granting and utilization of all cash advances to avoid problems in the future for its non-implementation.

We recommend that ELA or any similar activity of this nature in the future be conducted within the locality or province to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary expenses or waste of scarce government resources which is normally intended for the delivery of basic services to the LGU’s constituents.

We recommend the strict compliance in allocating 1% of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) as provided for under Section 15 of RA 9344 to ensure that the LGU’s Local Council for the Protection of Children can adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention, and to oversee its proper implementation.

Although it is a known
fact that LGU Talusan
like any other local
government units in
the country is facing
difficulty in raising
and accumulating the
necessary funds for it
to fully implement the
Solid Waste Management
Act of 2000 requiring
the complete stoppage
and closing of its
current practice of
operating an Open Dump
Site as a solid waste
management disposal
method like the
construction of the so
called Material
Recovery Facility as
well as a bio reactor
for the purpose and
the segregation of
solid waste at source,
still this is not in
accordance and
compliant with the
provisions of the said
law.
observations.managementAction:
On-going
compliance
Management
admitted
that it is
impossible
to create
a sanitary
landfill
due to its
high
costs.
However,
they
already
enacted
ordinances
for solid
wastes and
acquired
Material
Recovery
Facilities
and a Bio
reactor.
